outcomes.what_happened: Escalation kept building; ceasefire announced Apr 7 (day 7), oil −16% the next day.
Ultimatum window opened (Apr 8 deadline); ceasefire announced Apr 7.
Ceasefire announced the same day, after the morning reading; oil −16% in a day; score at 25 with backtrack detected by Apr 9.
outcomes.note: the flagship call — reading produced hours before the announcement

outcomes.what_happened: Post-backtrack cooldown held; no re-escalation within the horizon.
outcomes.evidence: June readings document the extended quiet

outcomes.what_happened: Cooldown deepened; zero Truth Social signals.
The market had already executed the reversal trade (oil −13.7%, S&P ATH) before the official signing; the flare faded to 22 by Jun 19.
outcomes.note: MEDIUM claims 'watch, not actionable' — no completed cycle event followed

outcomes.what_happened: Orderly cooldown continued; the reading estimated the cooldown ending ~Jul 10 — re-escalation began Jul 10.
outcomes.note: the cooldown-end estimate landed on the exact re-escalation date

outcomes.what_happened: Nothing tradable within the 7-day horizon; kinetic re-escalation began Jul 10 ('ceasefire is OVER').
outcomes.note: the reading refused to spike because oil was falling — correct restraint; its own note flagged the tariff front as the next trigger

outcomes.what_happened: Jul 21–22: the 20% Hormuz toll was dropped ('Gulf countries would instead invest in the US') — but the naval blockade proceeded, with CENTCOM redirecting vessels the next day.
outcomes.note: the reading gave ~55% tactical backdown within 48h vs ~35% escalation holds — the outcome split across exactly those two axes, as its own caveat warned

summary.caveat: n=10, single theme, no control group. Promising, not proven.
